member.name: Sarah Olney
member.synopsis: Sarah Olney is the Liberal Democrat MP for Richmond Park , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Business).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Turkey
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if artificial intelligence is used to assess ECAA applicants' extensions of their permissions to stay.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Artificial Intelligence is not used in applications made under Appendix ECAA for either indefinite leave to remain or further leave to remain.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department uses Artificial Intelligence in its decision-making process to assess applicants' rights to obtain a visa.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Where appropriate we use AI to improve productivity and effectiveness in our public services. All applications made under the Immigration Rules are considered and decided by trained human decision-makers.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: Tolls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to her Department's report entitled Draft Heathrow expansion national policy statement, published on June 2026, page 54, what discussions she has had with the Major of London on potential congest
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State has not met with the Mayor of London to discuss potential congestion charges to and from Heathrow Airport, in the context of Heathrow Expansion.

writtenQuestions.heading: Heathrow Airport: Tolls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of introducing a congestion charge in respect of journeys to and from Heathrow Airport.
writtenQuestions.answerText: None. The decision to introduce a road user charge is for the relevant traffic authorities for an area to make.

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Import Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what impact assessment he has made of the changes to steel tariffs on the manufacturing sector.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government engaged extensively with both primary steel producers and downstream users, including those in the manufacturing and defence sectors, to inform development of the steel trade measure, including a Call for Evidence in July 2025. The policy h...

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of excluding product codes 14 and 27 from the new steel tariffs.

writtenQuestions.heading: Airports: National Policy Statements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when she plans to publish the draft Airport National Policy Statement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Government’s October 2025 announcement, we are working to consult on the outcome of the Airports National Policy Statement (ANPS) review by the summer.

writtenQuestions.heading: Suicide: Mental Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what criteria he uses to assess the effectiveness of the Suicide Prevention Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data, evidence, and engagement with experts informed the actions that sit within the Suicide Prevention Strategy’s action plan. Government departments and other organisations responsible for delivering suicide prevention activity are accountable for the d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Suicide: Mental Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when the mid-term review of the Suicide Prevention Strategy will be published.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Officials are reviewing the Suicide Prevention Strategy, including how best to present updates, acknowledging the successes of the strategy so far as well as re-invigorating cross-Government efforts to go further. The Parliamentary Under-Secretary of Stat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much was spent on the Pharmacy First awareness campaign.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Pharmacy First advertising campaign has now run on several occasions. Evaluation of the November to December 2024 campaign showed a measurable increase in public awareness and confidence. Awareness that pharmacies can treat Pharmacy First conditions r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what discussions she has had with Cabinet colleagues on the adequacy of funding for domestic abuse services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Freedom from Violence and Abuse: a cross-government strategy committed to invest more than £1 billion over the next three years to support victims, including: £550 million for victims’ services from the Ministry of Justice;£499 million for safe accomm...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hammersmith Bridge: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she has held discussions with (a) Transport for London and (b) Hammersmith and Fulham Council on potential funding contributions for repairs for Hammersmith Bridge.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I wrote to you and members of the Hammersmith Bridge Taskforce, which includes Transport for London and Hammersmith and Fulham Council, on 27 February.I set out in my letter that I consider Hammersmith Bridge to be a good candidate for investment from the...

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many sites has her Department shortlisted to potentially receive funding from the Structure Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport surveyed local highway authorities and transport stakeholders on the assessment criteria for the Structures Fund in February 2026. We are currently considering the responses and will confirm the final prioritisation criteria i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what criteria will be used to consider potential sites for funding via the Structures Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport surveyed local highway authorities and other transport stakeholders on the criteria for prioritising structures for investment through the Structures Fund in February 2026. The Department is currently considering responses to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Oppression
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how does the Home Office learn of, or uncover, incidents of transnational repression.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We continually assess potential threats in the UK and draw on a multisource model to build a comprehensive picture of that threat.The Government works closely with law enforcement, government departments, international partners, civil society and affected...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign Influence Registration Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will set the processes by which the Foreign Influence Registration Scheme collects data from individuals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Those registering under FIRS do so through a dedicated IT platform. Comprehensive guidance on the scheme has been published on gov.uk. This includes guidance on what data needs to be registered and what data will appear on the public register.In addition,...

writtenQuestions.heading: Taxation: Interest Payments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how many residents have been charged interest on late payments to HMRC in each year since 2015.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We do not hold aggregated data on the total number of individuals who have paid late payment interest.

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of the capabilities of UK data centres to meet the energy usage required with the increasing use of AI.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government keeps UK data centre capability under review and works closely with energy regulators, network operators and industry.Through the AI Energy Council, Government is assessing system‑wide impacts, promoting energy efficiency and clean power so...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Redundancy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether he has made an assessment of the adequacy of redundancy plans for civil servants working at grades (a) AA/AO, (b) EO, (c) HEO/SEO, (d) G6/G7 and (e) SCS in each Government department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Each department will take a decision on its individual size and shape as per the financial settlements that have now been agreed with HMT in the Spending Review.
